james-settelmeyer: Establishment-backed rancher and ex-state Senate GOP leader; runs on low taxes, water rights, and border security.
david-flippo: Trump-endorsed retired Air Force colonel making illegal immigration and border security his central issue.
fred-simon-jr: Gardnerville trauma surgeon and perennial candidate stressing a balanced budget and energy/immigration policy.
tom-doyle: 45-year casino-industry veteran who would largely continue Amodei's record; backs DACA but opposes birthright citizenship.
george-forbush: Retired police officer pitching 50-year mortgages, merit-based immigration, and curbs on drug-industry price gouging.
jerry-olsen: Retired Army medic and ex-deputy taking no donations; pledges to vote his constituents' will, not Trump's line.
mike-smith: Retired Navy fighter pilot whose top priority is the national debt; would repeal the ACA.
sherman-tylawsky: 28-year-old Ph.D. candidate and think-tank founder favoring consumption taxes and fewer regulations.
jesse-watts: Ex-Eureka County sheriff who suspended his campaign for Settelmeyer; pitched a Nevada oil refinery and deregulation.
jennifer-billat: Land-development owner running primarily on public-lands issues and fuel independence from California.
andrea-lowe: 32-year-old volunteer firefighter and school board member running on healthcare, jobs, and younger leadership.
bruce-grego: Attorney and ex-South Lake Tahoe councilman campaigning against the 'administrative state' and unelected officials.
rick-shepherd: Former Bernie delegate running in the GOP primary; backs universal healthcare, abortion rights, and election skepticism.
teresa-benitez-thompson: Former Assembly majority leader and social worker; the field's most experienced Democrat, focused on cost of living.
greg-kidd: Self-funding venture investor and Benitez-Thompson's chief rival; runs on affordability and reining in data centers.
kathy-durham: Union rep and civics teacher backing Medicare for All and opposing corporate consolidation.
matthew-fonken: Ex-state Democratic Party director pushing term limits, a lobbying ban, and a congressional stock-trading ban.
morgan-wadsworth: 27-year-old anti-establishment consensus pick; backs Medicare for All, abolishing ICE, and opposing data centers.
gamy-enriquez: DoorDash driver who has faced homelessness; proposes a $1,300 UBI funded by Nevada minerals and a 'Green Marshall Plan.'
johnny-kerns: Retired homicide investigator and true-crime author focused on the economy and national debt.
gerold-gorman: 2022 congressional candidate with minimal documented 2026 activity; no stated positions.
josh-hebert: Physics Ph.D. who organized the anti-establishment coalition; withdrew to back Wadsworth on a money-in-politics platform.
mark-jolle: Anti-establishment coalition Democrat who withdrew without endorsing; name remains on the ballot.
samuel-white: Anti-establishment coalition Democrat who withdrew and endorsed Wadsworth; name remains on the ballot.
lisa-krasner: Incumbent SD16 senator and attorney naming wildfire, election integrity, and education as Nevada's top challenges.
monica-jaye-stabbert: Conservative talk-radio host challenging Krasner; centers cost of living, attainable housing, and local hiring.
michael-ginsburg: Reno NV Energy manager wary of transferable tax credits; pushes EV highway fees and housing zoning reform.
noah-bachman-dodge: Republican candidate for Assembly District 25 with limited public position record.
julie-butler: 30-year state executive touting deep budget expertise; flags infrastructure and fuel-supply vulnerability.
rich-harvey: Career fire chief making water scarcity and wildfire prevention his signature issues.
drew-ribar: Towing-association founder and government-meeting regular campaigning on accountability and constitutional literacy.
stacy-woodbury: Veteran state administrator pitching affordability, a DOGE-style audit, and tougher crime and immigration enforcement.
devon-reese: Sitting Reno councilmember pitching a Truckee River park; reversed in 2026 to back a data center moratorium.
kate-marshall: Ex-state treasurer and lieutenant governor; consistent data-center opponent making housing and fiscal management central.
kathleen-taylor: Lombardo-endorsed vice-mayor and fiscal hawk; the lone council vote against the data center moratorium.
eddie-lorton: Self-funded businessman on his fourth mayoral bid; would sell city property to cut debt and mandate 20% affordable housing.
tim-ross: Retired sheriff's sergeant calling out city fiscal dysfunction; skeptical of data centers, focused on public safety.
corinthia-yancey: Security-company owner wanting growth without draining water; conditional moratorium support and budget reform.
jesse-razo: Longtime Reno builder and repeat mayoral candidate running unfunded, raising population-growth concerns.
john-wayne-zink: Musician and IT consultant known as 'The Mayor' on YouTube; names city overspending as the key issue.
greg-nuttle: Downtown bartender who wound down his campaign; had focused on downtown development and congestion.
matt-johnson: Grassroots Ward 2 candidate stressing planning and neighborhood engagement over consultant-driven campaigns.
summer-pellett: Construction-firm COO and Ward 2 fundraising leader backed by development and trade interests.
vanessa-vaupel: Former VA chief of pharmacy running PAC-free; argues Reno deserves a deliberate data center pause with guardrails.
ian-luetkehans: Ward 2 council candidate running a minimal campaign with no funds or website; limited position record.
meghan-ebert: Incumbent Ward 4 councilmember insisting any data center moratorium be tied to codified protections.
eddie-gomez: North Valleys restaurateur challenging on infrastructure that has lagged growth, plus parks and affordable housing.
ed-lawson: Incumbent Sparks mayor pinning fiscal woes on public-safety costs; pushes property-tax reform and eastward land-bill growth.
charlene-bybee: Term-limited Sparks councilmember and self-styled fiscal conservative running on workforce housing and property-tax reset.
diana-mackey: Late-entry single-issue candidate opposing the Red Hawk golf-course development; blames deficit on tax abatements.
wendi-rawson: Urban-winery founder and outsider blaming the deficit on current leadership; opposes new resident taxes.
jamie-oconnell: Billing analyst and Democrat opposing the first-responder fee; would protect first responders, cut redundant contracts.
hunter-rand: Pirate Party broadcast engineer pushing infrastructure-first growth and scrutiny of surveillance-tech spending.
dian-vanderwell: Appointed Ward 2 incumbent and realtor favoring smart infill growth; cautious first-responder-fee support.
anthony-goulet: Sparks Ward 2 candidate who didn't respond to questionnaires and raised no funds; no stated positions.
damon-harrell: Marine veteran and realtor in his first race; protects public-safety funding and wants infrastructure before growth.
adam-mayberry: School board member leaving to run for council; prefers Medicaid reimbursement over a first-responder fee.
sean-mccaffrey: Bin-cleaning business owner fiercely opposing the first-responder fee; prioritizes reopening Fire Station 5.
tracey-thomas: Independent American first-timer prioritizing maintaining existing assets and higher-density town centers over sprawl.
joe-lombardo: Incumbent GOP governor and ex-Clark County sheriff touting 87 vetoes, school choice, and tougher immigration stances.
irina-hansen: Salon owner mounting a longshot GOP primary challenge to Gov. Lombardo; no stated positions.
matthew-winterhawk: Hip-hop artist running as a longshot GOP challenger to Gov. Lombardo; no stated positions.
aaron-ford: Term-limited AG and Democratic gubernatorial frontrunner running on lowering costs, housing, and data-center fairness.
alexis-hill: Washoe Commission chair and underdog Democrat; targets corporate tax giveaways and big K-12 and climate investment.
stavros-anthony: Incumbent GOP lieutenant governor and ex-LVMPD captain, unopposed in his primary.
sandra-jauregui: Assembly majority floor leader and Democratic LG frontrunner backed by Nevada's U.S. senators.
courtney-burke: Pediatric ICU nurse and small-business owner in the Democratic LG primary; no campaign funds reported.
bridget-scheiner: Veteran fire dancer in the Democratic LG primary, running on rejecting corporate PAC and lobbyist money.
nicole-cannizzaro: Senate majority leader and deputy DA touting sole courtroom experience in the Democratic AG primary.
zach-conine: Two-term state treasurer in the Democratic AG primary, running on fighting federal overreach and NV Energy rates.
adriana-guzman-fralick: Trump- and Lombardo-endorsed attorney in the GOP AG primary, campaigning on closer law-enforcement ties.
danny-tarkanian: Douglas County commissioner and perennial candidate with a famous name in the GOP AG primary.
cisco-aguilar: Incumbent Democratic secretary of state who advanced unopposed in his primary.
jim-marchant: Election-denying ex-assemblyman and 2020 'fake elector' who would eliminate voting machines.
sharron-angle: Former assemblywoman and 2010 GOP Senate nominee running on election-security doubts and voter ID.
shirley-folkins-roberts: Lombardo-endorsed attorney and developer backing voter ID and limits on mail-in ballots without alleging fraud.
socorro-keenan: Repeat SoS candidate claiming Nevada elections aren't secure and pushing to clean voter rolls.
joe-dalia: First-term Henderson assemblymember and attorney (ex-Meta) in the Democratic treasurer primary.
tya-mathis-coleman: Deputy state treasurer arguing her in-office experience lets her manage state finances from day one.
jay-maharjan: Teacher and entrepreneur who led the Obama-era Startup America Partnership, in the Democratic treasurer primary.
drew-johnson: Conservative think-tank founder running as a government-waste expert and budget watchdog for treasurer.
jeff-carter: Ex-Chicago Mercantile Exchange trader and first-time candidate in the GOP treasurer primary.
andy-matthews: Incumbent GOP controller and transparency advocate who built the state's online spending checkbook; unopposed.
robert-blackstock: Democratic candidate for Nevada State Controller with limited public position record.
michael-macdougall: Henderson high-school teacher running in the Democratic controller primary.
robert-tolle: Democratic candidate for Nevada State Controller with limited public position record.
mike-clark: Incumbent District 2 commissioner and ex-assessor; conservative on fiscal discipline, voter ID, and infrastructure-first growth.
jon-killoran: Sports-coalition CEO and former news director challenging Clark; backs voter ID and streamlined emergency dispatch.
tammy-holt-still: Flood-mitigation advocate energized by 2017 Lemmon Valley flooding; centers infrastructure, water, and transparency.
rob-pierce: Navy veteran and ex-planning commissioner running on responsible, pro-business growth; Builders Alliance endorsed.
katherine-yriarte: Horse trainer and Equine Council founder, Washoe GOP endorsed; balances open-space protection with development.
darin-balaam: Incumbent Washoe sheriff running unopposed in a now-nonpartisan race.
chris-hicks: Incumbent DA since 2014 citing top-in-state incarceration rates as proof his approach keeps the county safe.
wes-duncan: Sparks city attorney and ex-prosecutor, police-union and GOP backed, attacking Hicks's charging policy as win-rate-driven.
chris-sarman: Incumbent Republican Washoe County Assessor running unopposed.
justin-taylor: Incumbent Republican Washoe County Treasurer running unopposed.
nicole-klitzke: Incumbent Republican Washoe County Public Administrator, Washoe GOP endorsed; appears unopposed.
jan-galassini: Incumbent Republican Washoe County Clerk; appears uncontested in 2026.
kalie-work: Incumbent Democratic Washoe County Recorder since 2019; appears uncontested in 2026.
sheena-harvey: Education-doctorate community volunteer naming chronic absenteeism the top issue in the WCSD District F race.
brooke-westlake: Parent advocate and Washoe GOP-endorsed candidate prioritizing WCSD budget deficits, enrollment, and parent voice.
scott-benton: Candidate for WCSD Board District F with no confirmed biography or stated positions.
colleen-westlake: Incumbent WCSD Board trustee for District B running unopposed.
james-phoenix: Incumbent WCSD Board trustee for District C running unopposed.
